color.name: --color-action-primary
color.usage: Button primary variant's fill (default/disabled state).

color.name: --color-action-primary-hover
color.usage: Button primary variant's fill on hover.

color.name: --color-action-secondary
color.usage: Transparent — Button's secondary variant fill in its default state. Aliases Alpha/White-0 in Figma (confirmed 2026-07-19 — the Alpha scale is a real primitive collection in the file, just bound to no canvas node, which is why it wasn't found in the original sync's node-binding search). Not yet wired to a component via this token name (Secondary uses a literal bg-transparent); included for completeness of the Figma semantic layer.

color.name: --color-action-secondary-hover
color.usage: Aliases Alpha/White-10 in Figma (confirmed 2026-07-19). Unlike action-secondary above, this one has a real consumer: Button's secondary variant hover state (hover:bg-action-secondary-hover).

color.name: --color-action-highlight
color.usage: Button accent variant's fill in its default and focused states.

color.name: --color-action-highlight-hover
color.usage: Aliases Amber/200 in Figma — rebound from Amber/50 (#c07b0e) in a 2026-07-21 re-check found while building Button. Amber/50 measured only 4.02:1 against Accent's text-primary label, under WCAG 1.4.3's 4.5:1 minimum; Amber/200 clears it at 5.95:1. Button accent's Hover state binds to this directly.

color.name: --color-border-default
color.usage: Button secondary variant's resting border; also the resting border for every Input-family control (Input/Select/Textarea/Checkbox).

color.name: --color-border-focus
color.usage: Aliases Blue/100 in Figma — rebound from Amber/25 (#88570a) in a 2026-07-21 re-check found while building Button; moved in lockstep with state-focus's own Amber/25 -> Blue/100 rebind earlier that day. Button primary/secondary/link's Focused state and every Input-family control bind to this directly. Button accent's Focused state does NOT use this — see border-focus-on-highlight below, since Blue/100 fails WCAG 1.4.11 (1.47:1) against accent's action-highlight fill.

color.name: --color-border-subtle
color.usage: Hover-state border color for every Input-family control.

color.name: --color-border-strong
color.usage: Aliases Olive/50 in Figma. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-border-focus-inverse
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Aliases Amber/100 in Figma — confirmed directly by the token's creator during PR cross-review (2026-07-19), correcting an initial pattern-matched guess of Amber/400 (#eab460). Same value as action-highlight/action-primary-hover/text-highlight-inverse, kept distinct per convention. On the surfaces it was meant to fix: clears WCAG 1.4.11's 3:1 minimum on surface-inverse (5.3:1), surface-secondary (3.61:1), and surface-emphasis (5.37:1) — but still falls short on surface-feature (2.18:1, the Card CTA case). Not applied to Checkbox or Button by this sync.

color.name: --color-border-focus-on-highlight
color.usage: New 2026-07-21. Aliases Blue/25 in Figma. Button accent's Focused-state ring only — border-focus (Blue/100) measures 1.47:1 against accent's action-highlight fill, short of WCAG 1.4.11's 3:1 non-text minimum. This darker blue clears it at 3.96:1. Scoped to accent specifically; border-focus is unchanged everywhere else.

color.name: --color-text-primary
color.usage: Default body text color; also the typed-value color for Input-family controls when focused (see Input's placeholder-vs-value reconstruction).

color.name: --color-text-secondary
color.usage: Aliases Olive/100 in Figma. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-text-inverse
color.usage: Text/border color for content meant to sit on a dark or colored surface (Button secondary variant, Checkbox's label) — assumes a dark backdrop, see Checkbox's Storybook decorator for why.

color.name: --color-text-muted
color.usage: Placeholder text color for Input-family controls; also Checkbox's disabled-state checkmark/label color.

color.name: --color-text-highlight
color.usage: Checkbox's checkmark glyph. Aliases Amber/25 in Figma — changed from #df8e10 (Amber/100) in the 2026-07-19 token sync, so it no longer matches action-highlight/border-focus-hover the way it used to. Still kept as a distinct token from border-focus/state-focus even though the value is identical, per this file's own alias-not-collapse convention.

color.name: --color-text-link
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Aliases Amber/25 in Figma. Not yet used by any built component — Button's link variant binds to text-button/text-button-inverse instead; that's a real existing divergence, not something this sync changed.

color.name: --color-text-button
color.usage: Aliases Amber/25 in Figma. Went through two rebinds the same day while building Button: Amber/25 originally -> Amber/100 (#df8e10), which measured only 2.62:1 against white (and worse, ~2.2:1, against the system's actual light surfaces action-primary/surface-primary) — under WCAG 1.4.3's 4.5:1 minimum. Rebound back to Amber/25 once that was flagged, which clears AA everywhere checked (~5.1-6.2:1). Button link's Default state binds to this directly.

color.name: --color-text-link-inverse
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Aliases Amber/400 in Figma — the dark-surface counterpart of text-link. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-text-button-inverse
color.usage: Aliases Amber/400 in Figma. Went through two rebinds the same day while building Button: Amber/400 originally -> Amber/300 (#e6a745), then back to Amber/400 alongside text-button's own Amber/100 -> Amber/25 revert. Button link's Hover and Focused states both bind to this directly.

color.name: --color-text-highlight-inverse
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Aliases Amber/100 in Figma — originally back-solved by matching the Figma 'Surface Pairings' spec's stated contrast ratios (5.3:1 on surface-inverse, 5.4:1 on surface-emphasis) against the primitive ramp, since no node in the file had this token bound directly. Confirmed exactly right during PR cross-review (2026-07-19). Same value as action-highlight/action-primary-hover, kept distinct per convention. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-text-on-action
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Aliases Grey/100 in Figma — originally back-solved by matching the Figma 'Surface Pairings' spec's stated 11.8:1 ratio on action-primary against the primitive ramp, since no node in the file had this token bound directly. Confirmed exactly right during PR cross-review (2026-07-19). Same value as text-primary, kept distinct per convention. Button primary's Default/Hover/Focused label text binds to this directly (Disabled keeps text-primary — a real, separate binding, not collapsed just because the value matches).

color.name: --color-text-on-highlight
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Aliases Grey/100 in Figma — originally back-solved by matching the Figma 'Surface Pairings' spec's stated 5.3:1 ratio on action-highlight against the primitive ramp, since no node in the file had this token bound directly. Confirmed exactly right during PR cross-review (2026-07-19). Button accent's Default/Hover/Focused label text binds to this directly (Disabled keeps text-primary, same distinction as text-on-action above).

color.name: --color-text-on-feature
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Aliases Sand/500 in Figma — originally back-solved by matching the Figma 'Surface Pairings' spec's stated 4.9:1 ratio on surface-feature against the primitive ramp, since no node in the file had this token bound directly. Confirmed exactly right during PR cross-review (2026-07-19). Same value as text-inverse, kept distinct per convention (this is the dedicated feature-surface text token; text-inverse is the general dark-surface one). Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-surface-primary
color.usage: Default background fill for every Input-family control.

color.name: --color-surface-secondary
color.usage: Aliases Olive/50 in Figma. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-surface-tertiary
color.usage: Aliases Sand/900 in Figma. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-surface-inverse
color.usage: Aliases Grey/100 in Figma. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-surface-feature
color.usage: Aliases Terracotta/100 in Figma. Darkened from #c4582a to #a74b24 in the 2026-07-19 token sync so text-inverse reaches AA contrast on this surface (per the Figma Accessibility spec's note: 'Terracotta/100 was darkened from rgb(196,88,42) to rgb(167,75,36) so text-inverse passes').

color.name: --color-surface-emphasis
color.usage: Aliases Olive/25 in Figma. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-surface-scrim
color.usage: Aliases Grey/50 in Figma — for modal/overlay backdrops. Not yet used by any built component (no Modal built yet).

color.name: --color-surface-card
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Confirmed via a live Figma variable binding. Aliases Olive/75 (VariableID 242:2) — confirmed to exist during PR cross-review (2026-07-19); the initial sync had correctly inferred both the value and the ramp-step name from #4a5435 sitting almost exactly at the linear midpoint of Olive/50 (#3d4a2e) and Olive/100 (#5d6440), but had flagged it as unconfirmed pending designer sign-off. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-state-hover
color.usage: Background fill for Input-family controls on hover.

color.name: --color-state-disabled
color.usage: Aliases Olive/400 in Figma — rebound from Grey/500 (#8b8a88) in a 2026-07-21 re-check found while building Button. Border color for Input-family controls in the disabled state; also Button link's disabled-state label text.

color.name: --color-state-focus
color.usage: Aliases the new Blue/100 primitive in Figma — rebound 2026-07-21 from Amber/25 (#88570a), which state.focus previously shared with state.warning/border.focus by coincidence rather than real relation. Gives "focus" its own distinct color now that Badge's Warning variant has its own dedicated state.warning token. Not yet used by any built component directly — border.focus is what components reference for focus-ring styling, and it moved to Blue/100 too in a same-day re-check.

color.name: --color-state-warning
color.usage: Aliases Amber/25 in Figma. Badge's Warning variant background — Figma rebound this from state.focus to its own dedicated variable on 2026-07-20. Briefly moved to #c07b0e/Amber/50 in a 2026-07-21 re-check, which failed WCAG AA against text-inverse (2.94:1, needs 4.5:1, confirmed via Storybook's a11y addon on the Warning story); rebound back to Amber/25 in Figma the same day once that was flagged.

color.name: --color-state-success
color.usage: Aliases Green/100 in Figma — changed from the previously-unaliased #16a34a in the 2026-07-19 token sync, which also formalized Green/100 as a primitive. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-state-error
color.usage: Aliases Red/100 in Figma — changed from the previously-unaliased #dc2626 in the 2026-07-19 token sync, which also formalized Red/100 as a primitive. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-sand-100
color.usage: Full primitive-tier palette ramps (Figma's "Primitives" variable collection, 81 variables total as of the 2026-07-19 token sync — was 71, +Amber/25, +Olive/75, +Green/100, +Red/100, +6 Alpha steps). These sit underneath the semantic tokens above — some semantic tokens alias directly into these ramps (documented in the comments above), most of the ramp is not consumed by any built component yet. sand-400 is the one primitive with an existing, real consumer (Card).

color.name: --color-sand-200
color.usage: Sand palette — step 200 (2 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-sand-300
color.usage: Sand palette — step 300 (3 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-sand-400
color.usage: Primitive palette token; Card binds to this directly rather than a semantic text/surface token.

color.name: --color-sand-500
color.usage: Sand palette — step 500 (5 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-sand-600
color.usage: Sand palette — step 600 (6 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-sand-700
color.usage: Sand palette — step 700 (7 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-sand-800
color.usage: Sand palette — step 800 (8 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-sand-900
color.usage: Sand palette — step 900 (9 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-terracotta-100
color.usage: Darkened from #c4582a in the 2026-07-19 token sync so surface-feature keeps AA text contrast — see surface.feature's note. Steps 200-900 are unchanged.

color.name: --color-terracotta-200
color.usage: Terracotta palette — step 200 (2 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-terracotta-300
color.usage: Terracotta palette — step 300 (3 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-terracotta-400
color.usage: Terracotta palette — step 400 (4 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-terracotta-900
color.usage: Terracotta palette — step 900 (9 of 9 in the ramp).

color.usage: Rose palette — step 100 (1 of 9 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-rose-200
color.usage: Rose palette — step 200 (2 of 9 in the ramp).

color.usage: Rose palette — step 300 (3 of 9 in the ramp).

color.usage: Burgundy palette — step 100 (1 of 10 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-burgundy-150
color.usage: Burgundy palette — step 150 (2 of 10 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-burgundy-200
color.usage: Burgundy palette — step 200 (3 of 10 in the ramp).

color.usage: Burgundy palette — step 600 (7 of 10 in the ramp).

color.usage: Burgundy palette — step 700 (8 of 10 in the ramp).

color.usage: Burgundy palette — step 800 (9 of 10 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-burgundy-900
color.usage: Burgundy palette — step 900 (10 of 10 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-amber-25
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync — a darker amber step added specifically so text-highlight/border-focus/state-focus/text-link/text-button reach AA contrast on light surfaces (see the Figma Accessibility spec's note on this file). Named/labelled 'Amber/25' in Figma's own Full Token Registry documentation.

color.name: --color-amber-50
color.usage: Amber palette — step 50 (2 of 11 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-amber-100
color.usage: Amber palette — step 100 (3 of 11 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-amber-200
color.usage: Amber palette — step 200 (4 of 11 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-olive-25
color.usage: Olive palette — step 25 (1 of 12 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-olive-50
color.usage: Olive palette — step 50 (2 of 12 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-olive-75
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. VariableID 242:2, confirmed during PR cross-review (2026-07-19) — see surface.card's note for how the value was originally derived, ahead of that confirmation.

color.name: --color-olive-100
color.usage: Olive palette — step 100 (4 of 12 in the ramp).

color.usage: Grey palette — step 1000 (11 of 11 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-cream-50
color.usage: Renamed from Cream/200 in the 2026-07-19 token sync — same value (#ebe9e3), Figma re-numbered the ramp step. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-cream-100
color.usage: Cream palette — step 100 (2 of 2 in the ramp).

color.name: --color-green-100
color.usage: New primitive family in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Aliased by state.success.

color.name: --color-red-100
color.usage: New primitive family in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Aliased by state.error.

color.name: --color-blue-25
color.usage: New 2026-07-21. A darker blue step, same motivation as Amber/25's own history — added because Blue/100 fails WCAG 1.4.11 (3:1 non-text contrast) against mid-luminance fills like action-highlight. Aliased by border.focus-on-highlight.

color.name: --color-blue-100
color.usage: New primitive family, added 2026-07-21. Aliased by state.focus and border.focus.

color.name: --color-alpha-olive-10
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync (added later, after the initial sync missed it — it's bound to no canvas node in Figma, only readable directly from the Primitives variable collection). Olive/100 (#5d6440) at 10% opacity. Defined-but-unused: reserved for overlays and scrims, not text-bearing surfaces. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-alpha-olive-20
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Olive/100 (#5d6440) at 20% opacity. Defined-but-unused: reserved for overlays and scrims, not text-bearing surfaces. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-alpha-olive-40
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Olive/100 (#5d6440) at 40% opacity. Defined-but-unused: reserved for overlays and scrims, not text-bearing surfaces. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-alpha-olive-60
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. Olive/100 (#5d6440) at 60% opacity. Defined-but-unused: reserved for overlays and scrims, not text-bearing surfaces. Not yet used by any built component.

color.name: --color-alpha-white-0
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. White at 0% opacity. Aliased by action.secondary — see that token's note.

color.name: --color-alpha-white-10
color.usage: New in the 2026-07-19 token sync. White at 10% opacity. Aliased by action.secondary-hover — see that token's note. The one alpha step with a real component consumer (via action-secondary-hover).

typography.name: --text-h6
typography.usage: Button large-size label text; also the large-size value/label text for every Input-family control (Input/Select/Textarea/Checkbox).

typography.name: --text-paragraph-small
typography.usage: Card's date/time/description text; also the small-size value/label text for every Input-family control.

typography.name: --text-h3
typography.usage: Card's title text style — the only place the Recoleta display face is used.

spacing.name: --spacing-10
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

spacing.name: --spacing-00
spacing.usage: px, not rem: the host app sets root font-size to 18px (see index.css), which would otherwise scale every rem-based Tailwind utility 1.125x off its nominal value. Design tokens must stay pixel-exact regardless of the host's font-size choice. Repeated on every step below (not left to bleed across the ramp by adjacency) — see design-sync.js's extractCssTokenComments for why a comment no longer implicitly covers more than the one token directly beneath it.

spacing.name: --spacing-01
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

spacing.name: --spacing-02
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

spacing.name: --spacing-03
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

spacing.name: --spacing-04
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

spacing.name: --spacing-05
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

spacing.name: --spacing-06
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

spacing.name: --spacing-07
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

spacing.name: --spacing-08
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

spacing.name: --spacing-09
spacing.usage: px, not rem — see spacing-00's comment above for why.

radius.name: --radius-none
radius.usage: Overrides Tailwind's default rounded-none/xs/sm/md/lg/xl/2xl/full scale to match the full extracted radius ramp (Figma's "Corner Radius" collection, 8 steps). Repeated on every step below — see design-sync.js's extractCssTokenComments for why a comment no longer implicitly covers more than the one token directly beneath it.

radius.name: --radius-xs
radius.usage: Overrides Tailwind's default radius scale — see radius-none's comment above for why.

radius.name: --radius-sm
radius.usage: Overrides Tailwind's default radius scale — see radius-none's comment above for why.

radius.name: --radius-md
radius.usage: Overrides Tailwind's default radius scale — see radius-none's comment above for why.

radius.name: --radius-lg
radius.usage: Overrides Tailwind's default radius scale — see radius-none's comment above for why.

radius.name: --radius-xl
radius.usage: Overrides Tailwind's default radius scale — see radius-none's comment above for why.

radius.name: --radius-2xl
radius.usage: Overrides Tailwind's default radius scale — see radius-none's comment above for why.

radius.name: --radius-full
radius.usage: Overrides Tailwind's default radius scale — see radius-none's comment above for why.

motion.name: --duration-standard / --ease-standard
motion.usage: Not Figma-sourced (the file has no motion variables). Formalizes the identical transition-colors duration-150 ease-out already used in Button/Input/Select/Textarea/Checkbox.

breakpoint.name: --breakpoint-tablet
breakpoint.usage: Not Figma-sourced (no breakpoint variables exist in the file) and not yet used by any of the 6 built components (none are responsive). Formalizes the one breakpoint value already used repeatedly across the pre-existing app shell (src/index.css, src/App.css). Tailwind's own default scale (sm 640px / md 768px / lg 1024px / xl 1280px / 2xl 1536px) is available but not customized or formally adopted by this design system.

shadowNote: No shadow tokens are defined. The Figma source file has zero effect styles, and no component in this system currently uses box-shadow. This is an accurate empty state, not an omission — see the Shadows foundation page.
